senses.definition: used to imply that possessing wealth can provide people with significant advantages, influence, and control over others or situations

examples.x: The politician knew that to win the election, he needed to have a lot of money because, in politics, money is power.

senses.definition: used to highlight the power and influence of financial resources, as it can be used to influence or persuade people to act in a certain way or to achieve a desired outcome

examples.x: The wealthy businessman was able to get the best lawyers and avoid jail time because, as the saying goes, money talks.

senses.definition: used to suggest that the value of money is not affected by the way it is obtained or used

examples.x: The company didn't care about the ethics of their clients as long as they were making a profit because, to them, money has no smell.

word_list.word: money like manure, does no good (till|until) it is spread
word_list.pronunciation: mˈʌni lˈaɪk mənjˈʊɹ dˈʌz nˈoʊ ɡˈʊd tˈɪl ʌntˈɪl ɪt ɪz spɹˈɛd

senses.definition: used to suggest that accumulating wealth is not enough, and that one must put it to use in order to achieve positive outcomes, emphasizing the importance of using financial resources effectively

word_list.word: (love of|) money is the root of all evil
word_list.pronunciation: lˈʌv ʌv mˈʌni ɪz ðə ɹˈuːt ʌv ˈɔːl ˈiːvəl

senses.definition: used to imply that the love of money or excessive focus on wealth can lead to immoral or harmful actions

examples.x: The wealthy businessman was so obsessed with making more money that he cut corners and engaged in shady deals, proving that money is the root of all evil.

word_list.word: a golden key can open any door
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ ɡˈoʊldən kˈiː kæn ˈoʊpən ˌɛni dˈoːɹ

senses.definition: used to suggest that wealth or privilege can provide access to opportunities and success, implying that having resources and connections can be a significant advantage in achieving one's goals

examples.x: Despite her lack of experience, the young actress was able to secure a lead role in a Hollywood film because of her family's wealth and influence, showing that a golden key can open any door.

word_list.word: a great fortune is (also|) a great slavery
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ ɡɹˈeɪt fˈɔːɹtʃən ɪz ˈɑːlsoʊ ɐ ɡɹˈeɪt slˈeɪvɚɹi

senses.definition: used to convey the idea that having a significant fortune or immense wealth can often result in a loss of freedom, as it comes with numerous obligations, responsibilities, and dependencies

examples.x: Despite his immense wealth, John felt trapped by the constant demands and expectations of his social circle and business partners, demonstrating how a great fortune is a great slavery.

senses.definition: used to imply that when two forms of money are in circulation, one of higher value and one of lower value, people tend to hoard or use the money of lower value while spending or getting rid of the money of higher value

examples.x: When counterfeit bills started circulating in the town, people became skeptical of using the genuine currency, leading to a decrease in the overall value of money in circulation, showing how bad money drives out good.

word_list.word: beauty is potent, but money is omnipotent
word_list.pronunciation: bjˈuːɾi ɪz pˈoʊtənt bˌʌt mˈʌni ɪz ɑːmnˈɪpoʊtənt

senses.definition: used to imply that while beauty can be influential, money holds greater power and control over people and situations

word_list.word: he dances well to whom fortune pipes
word_list.pronunciation: hiː dˈænsᵻz wˈɛl tə hˈuːm fˈɔːɹtʃən pˈaɪps

senses.definition: used to imply that success in life is often easier to achieve for those who have access to favorable circumstances, such as wealth or opportunity
used to suggest that wealth or financial resources play a significant role in the success or stability of a marriage

examples.x: Some couples prioritize financial security above all else, believing that money makes marriage, but in doing so, they may neglect other important aspects of their relationship.

senses.definition: used to suggest that if one already has money or financial resources, it becomes easier to make more money through investments, business ventures, or other means

examples.x: The successful entrepreneur knew that money makes money and used his initial profits to fund new business ventures that eventually made him a millionaire.

senses.definition: used to suggest that in order to obtain something, one must be prepared to pay or work for it, as nothing in life comes for free

word_list.word: one who handles honey, licks his fingers
word_list.pronunciation: wˈʌn hˌuː hˈændəlz hˈʌni lˈɪks hɪz fˈɪŋɡɚz

senses.definition: used to imply that a person in charge of financial resources may be tempted to use them for personal gain and could potentially benefit financially as a result
used to imply that people who are wealthy or have a lot of money are often surrounded by many friends and acquaintances who seek to benefit from their wealth

examples.x: Although he was not particularly popular before his inheritance, he found that after his father's death, he suddenly had many new friends, illustrating that wealth attracts many friends.

word_list.word: a heavy purse makes (for|) a light heart
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ hˈɛvi pˈɜːs mˌeɪks fɔːɹ ɔːɹ ɐ lˈaɪt hˈɑːɹt

senses.definition: used to suggest that having a lot of money or financial security can relieve worries and bring happiness and peace of mind

examples.x: Although he was never obsessed with money, Jim acknowledged that a heavy purse makes a light heart, and he made sure to save for retirement.

senses.definition: used to suggest that finding an easy way to make money is a smarter choice than working hard

examples.x: The young man believed in the saying only fools and horses work, and spent all his time looking for get-rich-quick schemes instead of focusing on his education or career.

senses.definition: used to imply that having money or wealth can open up opportunities for further financial gain, such as through investment or other financial ventures

examples.x: "Although it may seem unfair, money begets money, and those who are born into wealth often have a head start in life," said the social commentator during the discussion.

word_list.word: a full purse makes the mouth to speak
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ fˈʊl pˈɜːs mˌeɪks ðə mˈaʊθ tə spˈiːk

senses.definition: used to suggest that wealth can be a source of power and influence, and that those who possess it may be more willing to share their thoughts or ideas with others
used to imply that wealth or money can be easily lost, emphasizing that financial success is not a guarantee of long-term security and that people should be careful with their money
used to suggest that those who are already successful or wealthy have an advantage in furthering their success, as they have access to resources and opportunities

examples.x: Sarah's father had many connections in the industry, and she found that much will have more, as she was able to secure a prestigious internship and gain valuable experience.
